fields.title: ralph resume can leave child session on inherited model instead of pinned execution profile
fields.description: Ralph loop iteration child sessions can start on the parent's inherited per-mode model assignment (e.g. moonshot/kimi-for-coding) even when .pi/loops/state/<task>.json pins a different execution profile (e.g. openai-codex/gpt-5.4). Evidence: controller session 2026-04-20T22-59-26-742Z... persists execution.modelsByMode.deep=moonshot/kimi-for-coding, while tau-audit-remaining state currently pins deep/openai-codex/gpt-5.4. Child session 2026-04-21T10-01-08-543Z... inherited the kimi modelsByMode snapshot and then ran assistant turns on provider moonshot/model kimi-for-coding without a correcting model_change entry. Root-cause hypothesis: commandBoundaryFromContext.applyExecutionProfile closes over the original command ctx and passes that stale ctx.model into PromptModes.applyExecutionProfile after ctx.newSession(). ExecutionRuntime.applyResolvedProfile uses readModelId(ctx.model) as the current-model check, so if the controller context already reports the pinned model, the apply becomes a no-op even though the active child session has different live model state after newSession. Ralph currently relies on sessionManager.getSessionFile() being live, but ctx.model is snapshot-like and does not reliably describe the post-newSession child session. Add a regression that resumes a loop with controller ctx.model matching the pinned profile while the child session inherits a different model, and assert Ralph still forces the child to the pinned execution profile.
